chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD Criminal Bail Application No.S-738 of 2023 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on office objections. 2. For hearing of main case. 11.09.2023 Mr. Ghulam Murtaza Leghari, Advocate for applicants along-with applicants (on bail). Ms. Rameshan Oad, Assistant Prosecutor General, Sindh. Complainant is present in person. == O R D E R ZULFIQAR ALI SANGI, J- Through the instant criminal bail application, the applicants/accused seek their pre-arrest bail in Crime No.58 of 2023, registered under sections 382, 506, 504 and 34 P.P.C, at P.S Kario Ganhwar, after their bail plea was declined by the learned 2nd Additional Sessions Judge Badin, vide order dated 12.07.2023. At the very outset, learned counsel for the applicants submits that though there is a dispute in between the parties on land matter; however, if the complainant is satisfied then they will give him an amount of Rs.12,500/- for two mounds of mustered oil (Toriyo). Complainant Ghulam Murtaza present in Court is agreed and has received the said amount and further submits that applicants are issuing threats, hence they be directed not to cause any harm to the complainant. Applicants also claim issuing of threats by complainant, as such, both parties are undertake that from today onwards they will reside in the vicinity peacefully and will not cause any harm to each other. Learned Assistant Prosecutor General, Sindh looking the development made in the matter formally opposed the confirmation of bail. In such circumstances and in view of the fact that applicants / accused has joined the investigation and they are no more requires for further probe.

Learned Assistant Prosecutor General, Sindh looking the development made in the matter formally opposed the confirmation of bail. In such circumstances and in view of the fact that applicants / accused has joined the investigation and they are no more requires for further probe. Further there is no allegation of misusing concession of interim pre-arrest bail by the applicants as they are attending the Trial Court regularly. At bail stage only tentative assessment is to be made, therefore, learned counsel for the applicants is made out a case for confirmation of interim pre-arrest bail. Resultantly bail application is allowed and ad-interim pre- arrest bail earlier granted to the applicants vide order dated 18.07.2023 is confirmed on the same terms and conditions. However the parties may proceed with the respective matters before the learned Trial Court without prejudice to the observations made in this bail application. JUDGE Muhammad Danish*
